diagnosis: This level fixed the previous flatness problem with enemies and hazard gaps, and it paid off: the player rated it 5, fought through 8 kills and 6 hits, and had two real falls near spawn (x70,y388) and mid-level (x1267,y318) where the floor gaps sit. Despite that, the level is still architecturally a single ground-level corridor with holes in it — enemies and gaps add danger and pacing, but there's no actual elevation change for the player to climb or descend, which is exactly what the comment flags as 'flat' even while calling it fun.
lessons: Enemies and hazard density can make a level feel fun and engaging even when the layout has no vertical variation — fun and 'verticality' are judged as separate axes by players.

Horizontal floor gaps/pits are not perceived as verticality by players even if platforms sit at different heights — true verticality needs traversal that changes the player's elevation (climbing/descending), not just gaps at one baseline.
save_reason: High rating with strong combat engagement (8 kills, real risk from falls) shows enemy/hazard density works well as a baseline layer to build verticality onto.
